mapper.select: select e.id, e.name, e.gender, e.age, e.phone, e.salary, e.entrydate as entryDate, e.create_time as createTime, e.update_time as updateTime, e.dept_id as deptId, d.name as deptName from emp e left join dept d on e.dept_id = d.id

select e.id, e.name, e.gender, e.age, e.phone, e.salary, e.entrydate as entryDate, e.create_time as createTime, e.update_time as updateTime, e.dept_id as deptId, d.name as deptName from emp e left join dept d on e.dept_id = d.id where e.id = #{id}
mapper.insert: insert into emp( name, gender, age, phone, salary, entrydate, dept_id, create_time, update_time ) values( #{name}, #{gender}, #{age}, #{phone}, #{salary}, #{entryDate}, #{deptId}, now(), now() )
mapper.update: update emp set name = #{name}, gender = #{gender}, age = #{age}, phone = #{phone}, salary = #{salary}, entrydate = #{entryDate}, dept_id = #{deptId}, update_time = now() where id = #{id}
mapper.select: select e.id, e.name, e.gender, e.age, e.phone, e.salary, e.entrydate as entryDate, e.create_time as createTime, e.update_time as updateTime, e.dept_id as deptId, d.name as deptName from emp e left join dept d on e.dept_id = d.id where e.name like concat('%', #{name}, '%')

select e.id, e.name, e.gender, e.age, e.phone, e.salary, e.entrydate as entryDate, e.create_time as createTime, e.update_time as updateTime, e.dept_id as deptId, d.name as deptName from emp e left join dept d on e.dept_id = d.id limit #{offset}, #{size}
